claim: Hybrid architecture: use frontier models (Opus, GPT-4) for coding and complex planning, offload embeddings, transcription, classification, and chat to local open-source models
actionable: Identify non-critical tasks (embeddings, voice, classification, simple chat) and run them on local models like Qwen, NeMo Tron, or Llama; reserve frontier models for coding and orchestration

claim: Open-source models handle 90% of use cases adequately; frontier models are only necessary for cutting-edge tasks
actionable: For routine tasks (embeddings, transcription, PDF extraction, classification, basic chat), use quantized open-source 30-40B models instead of frontier models to reduce costs

claim: Three-phase optimization: (1) Experiment with frontier models, (2) Productionize while identifying replaceable tasks, (3) Scale by replacing tasks with local models
actionable: Start all new agentic workflows using frontier models to validate they work; once reliable, identify which tasks can run locally and progressively migrate them

claim: LM Studio is the simplest tool to run open-source models locally; it determines which models fit your hardware and provides a web interface
actionable: Use LM Studio to deploy and manage local open-source models; it automatically selects compatible models based on your GPU VRAM and provides a simple interface

claim: SSH allows you to use remote GPUs (RTX 5090, DGX Spark) as external compute from your main machine running the AI agent system
actionable: Configure SSH access to your RTX/GPU machines; point your agent system (running on your main computer) to remote models via SSH for distributed inference

claim: Running embeddings locally keeps data private; replacing cloud embeddings with local models prevents data from leaving your office
actionable: For sensitive data, always run embeddings and RAG locally rather than sending data to cloud APIs to maintain privacy and security

claim: Open-source ASR (Whisper) and text-to-speech models run free locally vs $0.02+ per minute from OpenAI, saving hundreds per month at scale
actionable: Replace cloud ASR/TTS APIs with local Whisper or NeMo TTS models to cut speech processing costs from ~$12-20/month to near-zero electricity

claim: Qwen 3.5 35B with 3B active parameters generates 1,000-word stories 2-3x faster than Sonnet 4.6 running in the cloud
actionable: For text generation tasks where latency matters, test local open-source models (Qwen, NeMo, Llama); local inference often matches cloud quality with better speed
